rationale: Physics is one of the cleanest subdiscipline trees in science — arXiv's physics categories are basically the ground truth. I followed arXiv's structure plus astronomy/astrophysics (split as itself because the community is large). Condensed Matter is the biggest single bucket (~25% of physics papers) so I left it un-split rather than fight the picker space — interested readers will pick CM and then drill via the deep taxonomy. I split Quantum Information from theoretical CS/cryptography because quantum information physicists strongly identify here (and arXiv quant-ph confirms it).

subspecialties.name: Condensed Matter Physics
covers: electronic structure, superconductivity, magnetism, topological materials, 2D materials physics, strongly correlated systems
Phys Rev B, Nat Phys cond-mat

subspecialties.name: High Energy Physics
covers: particle physics, LHC and collider experiments, BSM theory, neutrino physics, dark matter direct detection
Phys Rev D, JHEP, arXiv hep-*

subspecialties.name: Atomic, Molecular & Optical Physics
subspecialties.tagline: Lasers, cold atoms, quantum optics, photonics.
covers: cold atom physics, quantum optics, laser physics, ultrafast and attosecond, precision measurement, photonics physics
Phys Rev A, Optica, Nat Photonics

subspecialties.name: Quantum Information & Quantum Computing
subspecialties.tagline: Qubits, error correction, simulation, quantum sensing.
covers: quantum hardware (superconducting, trapped ion, photonic), error correction, fault tolerance, quantum sensing, quantum simulation, quantum supremacy/advantage
PRX Quantum, Quantum, arXiv quant-ph
explicitly_excludes: quantum algorithms theory → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Astrophysics
covers: stellar astrophysics, galactic and extragalactic, exoplanets, multi-messenger astronomy, time-domain astronomy, AGN
ApJ, MNRAS, A&A, arXiv astro-ph

subspecialties.name: Cosmology
subspecialties.tagline: Early universe, dark energy, CMB, large-scale structure.
covers: early universe, inflation, dark matter cosmology, dark energy, CMB analysis, large-scale structure, JWST cosmological observations
JCAP, MNRAS cosmology
explicitly_excludes: particle dark matter direct detection → High Energy Physics

subspecialties.name: Planetary & Solar Physics
covers: planetary science, heliophysics, solar physics, planetary atmospheres, solar system exoplanetary comparison
Icarus, Sol Phys, JGR Planets

subspecialties.name: Nuclear Physics
covers: nuclear structure, nuclear reactions, hadron physics, QCD at low energy, nuclear astrophysics overlap
PRC, Nucl Phys A, arXiv nucl-*

subspecialties.name: Statistical & Nonlinear Physics
subspecialties.tagline: Stat mech, complex systems, soft matter, biophysics.
covers: statistical mechanics, nonlinear dynamics, complex networks, soft matter physics, active matter, biological physics from physicists' angle
Phys Rev E, J Stat Mech

subspecialties.name: Plasma Physics
covers: fusion plasmas, magnetic confinement, ICF, space plasmas, laboratory plasma physics, plasma astrophysics
Phys Plasmas, Nucl Fusion

subspecialties.name: Gravitation & Relativity
subspecialties.tagline: GR theory, gravitational waves, black holes.
covers: general relativity, gravitational waves (theory and data), black hole physics, modified gravity, quantum gravity
Phys Rev D gravitation, CQG, arXiv gr-qc

subspecialties.name: Fluid Mechanics
covers: turbulence theory, microfluidics physics, biological fluid mechanics, geophysical fluid dynamics, complex fluids
JFM, Phys Rev Fluids
explicitly_excludes: engineering flow modeling → Mechanical & Aerospace Engineering

subspecialties.name: Applied & Instrumentation Physics
covers: detector technology, particle accelerator physics, vacuum technology, precision sensors, applied superconductivity
Rev Sci Instrum, NIM A

uncovered_concerns: Considered separating Soft Matter from Statistical/Nonlinear — folds in there as the natural home.
Considered Gravitational-Wave Astronomy as its own card — overlap between Gravitation & Relativity and Astrophysics; let users pick the angle they identify with.
Considered Mathematical Physics — sits at the Physics/Math border; mostly under Statistical Physics or Gravitation here, also has a home in Mathematics.
